TEXT.source: NTU Corpus stories for Kanakanavu, story: kkvNr_sad_song_Muu
TEXT.citation: Sung, L. M., Lily, I., Hsieh, F., & Lin, Z. (2008). Developing an online corpus of Formosan languages. Taiwan Journal of Linguistics, 6(2.).
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @article{sung2008developing,title={Developing an online corpus of Formosan languages.},author={Sung, Li-May and Lily, I and Hsieh, Fuhui and Lin, Zhemin and others},journal={Taiwan Journal of Linguistics},volume={6},number={2},year={2008}}

TRANSL.notes: We met for the first time (recently).

S.TRANSL: We met for the first time .
我原本已經通過藤橋了.
I have originally gone through the rattan bridge.

TRANSL.notes: It's already dawn. (lit. It already became bright.)
I feel sorry, (because) I have been drinking.

S.TRANSL: I feel sorry, I have been drinking.

S.FORM: nomani kusai 'esi sipara sa mamariUpU unee tapunia.
S.PHON: nomani kusai ʔesi sipar~ɾa sa mamar~ɾiupu unee tapunia.
S.FORM: nomani kusai 'esi sipara sa mamariUpU unee tapunia.
S.PHON: nomani kusai ʔesi sipar~ɾa sa mamar~ɾiupu unee tapunia.

TRANSL.notes: 究竟是誰在對岸那裏揮手?(看到)白色的紅隼鳥.

S.TRANSL: 究竟是誰在對岸那裏揮手? 白色的紅隼鳥.

TRANSL.notes: Who exactly is waving hands on the opposite bank? (I see) a white red-billed eagle.

S.TRANSL: Who exactly is waving hands on the opposite bank? a white red-billed eagle.
It seems to be the father of a white red-billed eagle.
我們兄弟姊妹們首次會面.

TRANSL.notes: We brothers and sisters met for the first time (recently).

S.TRANSL: We brothers and sisters met for the first time .
請隨興地唱、請隨興地喝已經摻過水的.

TRANSL.notes: Please sing and drink (the wine) mixed with water.

S.TRANSL: Please sing and drink mixed with water.

W.TRANSL: 歌唱-主焦-喝-重疊-隨興-主焦.祈使
